questions.question: What is the main concept of an entity type in conceptual data modelling?
options: A) A group of related attributes
C) A group of related entities
questions.explanation: An entity type represents a real-world concept or object that has its own set of attributes.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a weak entity type?
questions.explanation: A weak entity type is an entity that is dependent on another entity for its existence.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an ER diagram in conceptual data modelling?
options: A) To visually represent the structure of a database
B) To create a physical database schema
C) To identify relationships between entities
questions.explanation: An ER diagram is used to visually represent the structure of a database and identify relationships between entities.

questions.question: What is the term for a group of entities that share common attributes?
questions.explanation: An entity set is a group of entities that share common attributes and are related to each other through relationships.

questions.question: What is a structural constraint in conceptual data modelling?
options: A) A rule that restricts the values of an attribute
B) A rule that defines the relationships between entities
C) A rule that ensures data consistency
D) A rule that defines data integrity
questions.explanation: A structural constraint is a rule that ensures data consistency and defines the relationships between entities.
